headline: Anthropic's Claude Fable 5, the first Mythos class model, dominates the cycle as educators test its benchmarks, pricing, and agentic uses.
body: The recommended channels converge on Claude Fable 5: Better Stack reports it tops SWE Bench Pro and Frontier Code with vision, long context, and web interaction [9], while Cole Medin stress tests whether its 2x price premium over Opus 4.8 is justified on real tasks [2]. Cyril Imhof frames Fable 5 as a second brain covering loop engineering for recursive self-improvement, memory systems, and a "Know Your Agent" context methodology for regulated, long-running applications [1][10], and IBM Technology pairs the model review with Apple's WWDC 2026 pivot to NVIDIA cloud inference over on-device processing [0]. Supporting threads include NVIDIA's Nemotron 3 Nano, Super, and Ultra models built on hybrid Mamba transformer, MTP, and Latent MoE [5], Groq's Jonathan Ross arguing cheaper inference fuels endless compute demand via Jevons paradox [3], and tooling like Google's Agents CLI with sandboxed execution and audit trails [6] plus PAI's persistent memory Life OS on Claude Code [7]. IBM Security examines prompt injection, AI worms, and unaudited nonhuman identities as enterprise risks [8].
